I had two friends who were both copywriters and one of them was a very brilliant tactical copywriter and the other was a reasonably competent strategic thinker. The tactical person got an idea many years ago that he wanted to go into the cubic zirconium that simulated diamond business so he wrote a brilliant ad for a company called the Beverly Hills Diamond Company and he created a proprietary name not unlike we talked about the Beverly Hills Diamond which was a simulated diamond and he wrote an incredibly compelling piece of copy and he ran it full page ad in the Los Angeles time and I think it cost 30 grand and it brought in 42 or something which after all expenses he made 5 or 6 grand and he was used to much bigger paydays. He got bored and he folded his tent and took his ball and left. Friend two who was not as good a copywriter but was a much more strategic thinker, oh by the way friend one before he took his tent down he would send out this in a stymie in the cheapest little package with no note just send it out because he didn't really deal with it as a tactical thinker. Friend two was totally strategic but not as good a copywriter. He sat down and wrote his own ad and he was more clever. This is back when Van Cleef and Arpels were real popular and Tiffany's was in real great vogue so he created a company called Van Pliss and Tissany and he created the Van Pliss Diamond and he wrote an ad that wasn't as good but it was serviceable and he spent the same $30,000 and as I recall he brought in like $28,000 or so on paper he lost 2 grand but he was not a tactical thinker. He was totally strategic. When he fulfilled the one carat $39 stone which everyone was buying it came first of all in a gorgeous velvet jeweler's bag inside a simulated wooden box that cost him a lot of money. It had two documents enclosed one was a letter and one was a brochure. The letter thanked him profoundly for investing in the Van Pliss Diamond and then it preemptively addressed the fact that when they opened the jeweler's bag and took out the stone two things would be evident. First it was infinitely more fiery brilliant just spectacular than they thought and second it was going to look smaller. It wasn't that he had cheated them it's that the Van Pliss Diamond had more density and so weighed more per millimeter and it said and when our clients see how magnificent the stone is the vast majority contact us because they want to get larger ones but then when they get them they have another problem they got to get them sat and they go to their jeweler's the jeweler's charge them an arm and a leg so as a courtesy to our valued clients we have taken the liberty of setting some absolutely spectacular 5, 10, 15 carat stones in beautiful necklaces rings and we've tried in 14, 18 carat gold we've priced them in approximately half of what the average jeweler would charge and you're welcome to try but because of the inconvenience and our appreciation we would like to extend you the chance to trade in the stone you've got for double credit towards anything you want to buy. Now he was a strategic thinker friend one was a tactical very talented copywriter friend one made about seven thousand dollars and quit friend two lost two thousand dollars and fifteen months later made twenty five million that's the difference between thinking strategically and tax.
